backlog.summary: Git-native task and project management via Backlog.md CLI, tracks tasks as markdown files in repos
backlog.scope_rationale: Task management is useful across all projects; the backlog data lives per-repo so no cross-project leakage
backlog.risk_notes: Low risk. Reads/writes markdown files in the current repo only.

cerebro-mcp.summary: Organizational knowledge base — remember, recall, and search across ADRs, SOPs, and topics
cerebro-mcp.scope_rationale: Organizational memory is relevant across all projects; provides context for architectural decisions everywhere
cerebro-mcp.risk_notes: Contains institutional knowledge and architectural decisions. Internal only.

claude-resume.summary: Session recovery for Claude Code — find, inspect, and resume crashed or abandoned sessions
claude-resume.scope_rationale: Session recovery is needed everywhere; reads local ~/.claude session data regardless of project
claude-resume.risk_notes: Reads Claude Code session transcripts which may contain sensitive code and conversations from any project.

context7.summary: Up-to-date library and framework documentation for LLMs, powered by Upstash
context7.scope_rationale: Documentation lookup is universally useful and contains no project-specific data
context7.risk_notes: Sends library names to Upstash servers for doc retrieval. No sensitive data transmitted. API key used for rate limits.

director-daemon.summary: Autonomous orchestrator for recurring personal work — comms extraction, security monitoring, scheduling
director-daemon.scope_rationale: Personal orchestration daemon relevant to all work contexts; aggregates comms and alerts
director-daemon.risk_notes: Accesses personal communications and security data. High sensitivity — internal only.

eidos.summary: Eidos AGI platform knowledge base — CLI guides, railway guides, vault guides, and cross-service search
eidos.scope_rationale: Only relevant to Eidos AGI projects; provides platform-specific documentation and search
eidos.risk_notes: Low risk. Read-only knowledge base for Eidos platform conventions and guides.

eidos-book-forge.summary: Composable book publishing pipeline — markdown in, formatted books out
eidos-book-forge.scope_rationale: Only needed when working on book/publishing projects; no tools registered in current session
eidos-book-forge.risk_notes: Low risk. Generates book artifacts from markdown content.

eidos-consent.summary: Billing-aware API key broker for the Eidos forge toolchain — manages billing entity selection and consent
eidos-consent.scope_rationale: Only needed for Eidos forge tools that require API key brokering; not relevant to non-Eidos projects
eidos-consent.risk_notes: Manages API keys and billing selection. Sensitive — controls which billing entity pays for AI API calls.

eidos-elt-forge.summary: Modular agentic framework for data pipelines — stateful workflow engine with medallion architecture
eidos-elt-forge.scope_rationale: Only relevant when building data pipelines; accesses database schemas and ETL configs
eidos-elt-forge.risk_notes: Accesses database schemas and can generate ETL pipeline code. Use only in data engineering contexts.

eidos-image-forge.summary: AI image generation via Google Gemini — cover art, illustrations, icons, and image editing
eidos-image-forge.scope_rationale: Image generation is useful across creative/design projects but not needed for pure backend work
eidos-image-forge.risk_notes: Sends prompts to Google Gemini for image generation. Generated images stored locally. API costs apply.

eidos-mail.summary: Email management — inbox, search, read, send, reply, forward, and sync for personal email
eidos-mail.scope_rationale: Email access is needed across all work contexts; personal communications are project-agnostic
eidos-mail.risk_notes: HIGH SENSITIVITY. Full email access — read, send, delete. Can compose and send emails on behalf of the user.

eidos-test-forge.summary: Point-and-test framework — probes HTTP, health, CLI, SQL, and MCP endpoints with iterative depth
eidos-test-forge.scope_rationale: Testing is relevant to development projects but should be scoped to avoid probing unrelated services
eidos-test-forge.risk_notes: Can probe live services and databases. Ensure it only targets intended environments.

elt-forge.summary: Standalone ELT forge instance — same as eidos-elt-forge but with separate Python environment
elt-forge.scope_rationale: Duplicate of eidos-elt-forge with different environment; only needed in specific data pipeline projects
elt-forge.risk_notes: Same as eidos-elt-forge. Consider consolidating to avoid confusion.

github.summary: GitHub's official MCP server — repos, issues, PRs, projects, code search via Copilot OAuth
github.scope_rationale: GitHub access is fundamental to all development work; OAuth scopes control actual access
github.risk_notes: Full GitHub access via PAT. Can create repos, push code, merge PRs, write issues. Token in config has broad scope.

helios.summary: Browser automation for AI agents — control logged-in browser sessions with clicks, screenshots, and navigation
helios.scope_rationale: Browser automation is powerful but dangerous; should only be enabled for projects that need web interaction
helios.risk_notes: HIGH SENSITIVITY. Controls real browser with logged-in sessions. Can see passwords, cookies, and interact with any website.

keeper.summary: Password management — search, retrieve, add, update, and retire credentials from a password vault
keeper.scope_rationale: Credential management is needed across all projects; provides secure access to passwords and API keys
keeper.risk_notes: CRITICAL SENSITIVITY. Full access to password vault. Can read, create, update, and delete credentials.

outlook.summary: Microsoft Outlook email and calendar — read inbox, compose, reply, search, and check calendar from local cache
outlook.scope_rationale: Work email and calendar access is needed across all projects; reads from local Outlook cache
outlook.risk_notes: HIGH SENSITIVITY. Reads Outlook local cache — all business emails and calendar. Can compose and reply to emails.

pal.summary: Provider Abstraction Layer — multi-LLM orchestration enabling Claude to consult GPT, Gemini, Grok, and others
sensitive_data_types: code snippets sent to external LLMs
pal.scope_rationale: Multi-model consultation is useful everywhere; the user's CLAUDE.md mandates consulting other models
pal.risk_notes: Sends code and conversation context to external LLM providers (OpenAI, Google, etc.). Data leaves local environment.

railguey.summary: Railway deployment management — deploy, rollback, logs, domains, variables, and service orchestration
railguey.scope_rationale: Railway is the standard deployment platform for all services; needed everywhere for deploys
railguey.risk_notes: Full Railway access — can deploy, rollback, delete projects, and read environment variables including secrets.

reeves-comms.summary: Unified communications aggregator — search, thread, and surface high-attention items across all channels
reeves-comms.scope_rationale: Communications are cross-cutting; relevant in any work context for checking messages and alerts
reeves-comms.risk_notes: Accesses aggregated communications from multiple channels. Contains business and personal messages.

reeves-global.summary: Content publishing pipeline — article intake, drafting, editing, and publishing to multiple destinations
reeves-global.scope_rationale: Article publishing is only relevant for content/marketing work, not general development
reeves-global.risk_notes: Can publish content to external destinations. Ensure articles are reviewed before pushing.

reeves-messages.summary: Apple Messages (iMessage/SMS) management — conversations, search, drafts, identity resolution, and sweep
reeves-messages.scope_rationale: Personal messaging is cross-cutting; identity resolution and message management needed in any context
reeves-messages.risk_notes: HIGH SENSITIVITY. Reads all Apple Messages. Can draft replies and manage message identities.

reeves-view.summary: Always-on view daemon — push, pin, and restore named views with SSE real-time updates
reeves-view.scope_rationale: View management daemon is infrastructure-level; relevant across all projects for displaying data
reeves-view.risk_notes: Low risk. Manages display views. No sensitive data access — just layout and rendering.

rhea-diagrams.summary: AI-powered diagramming via Excalidraw — create, edit, export diagrams with brand themes and Mermaid import
rhea-diagrams.scope_rationale: Diagramming is useful across all projects for architecture docs, flowcharts, and visual communication
rhea-diagrams.risk_notes: Low risk. Creates diagrams locally. No external data transmission.

taskr.summary: Full-stack work management — tasks, devlogs, skillflows, governance, DNS, secrets (Knox), projects, and recon missions
taskr.scope_rationale: Central work management system; tasks, devlogs, and governance span all projects
taskr.risk_notes: HIGH SENSITIVITY. Can execute SQL queries, manage secrets via Knox, modify DNS records, and approve governance decisions.

vercel.summary: Vercel deployment platform — project metadata, logs, and deployment status (read-only)
vercel.scope_rationale: Only relevant for projects deployed to Vercel; not all projects use Vercel
vercel.risk_notes: Read-only access to Vercel projects and logs. OAuth-scoped. Low risk but exposes deployment details.

wrike.summary: Wrike project management — tasks, comments, folders, spaces, and unified search across the AIC workspace
wrike.scope_rationale: Wrike contains AIC client and business data; should only be available in AIC work contexts
wrike.risk_notes: Accesses AIC business Wrike workspace. Contains client project data, task assignments, and business communications.
